title: Weekend Box Office: Alien: Romulus Dethrones Deadpool & Wolverine
text: Director Fede Alvarez's horror film "Alien: Romulus" claimed the top spot at the box office with a $41.5 million opening weekend, ending "Deadpool & Wolverine's" four-week reign. The superhero sequel remained in second place, adding $29 million and achieving a historic milestone as "the highest-grossing R-rated film ever worldwide." The article notes that "Romulus" achieved the 17th-best August opening, positioning it as the third-strongest debut in the "Alien" franchise's history. With an $80 million budget and $108 million in global earnings, the film aims to surpass "Prometheus's" performance while avoiding the word-of-mouth challenges that plagued earlier installments. "Deadpool & Wolverine" surpassed $545 million domestically and $1.143 billion globally, placing it on track for $630-650 million at home. Meanwhile, "It Ends with Us" continued strong performance with $24 million, reaching $97.8 million total. The article also highlights theatrical disappointments, including "Borderlands", which experienced a 73% drop—the year's largest decline for wide releases—and "Twisters", struggling internationally despite strong domestic numbers.
